compositional_truth_check.P2: Systemic Loss (No evidence of Mexican Mint)
compositional_truth_check.P4: Systemic Loss (Shared space with P2 is barren)

inventory_reconciliation.notes: P2 and P4 are currently devoid of their registered specimens. The soil in the lower black pot contains only a single, isolated, possibly adventitious leaf/sprout, which does not match the expected Mexican Mint or Silver Guest morphology.

plant_audit.P1: String of Nickels: Stable, showing consistent foliage density.
plant_audit.P3: Pothos: Significant chlorosis and necrosis on the primary leaf. The white rabbit anchor remains stable.
plant_audit.P2_P4_Zone: The lower pot shows a single, lanceolate green leaf. This is likely a new, uncatalogued sprout or a remnant of a previous planting, not the registered species.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Surface appears dry with visible perlite/debris. No fungal blooms detected.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Clean, no significant debris or spills noted.
biome_observations.incidental_growth: One small, unidentified sprout in the lower black pot.

temporal_deltas.T-5_to_T-1: Gradual decline in P3 leaf health; the yellowing/browning of the large Pothos leaf has progressed.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_CURRENT: Stasis in P3; the lower pot sprout remains unchanged in posture.

vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical audit reveals a concerning decline in the Pothos (P3), characterized by progressive leaf browning. The Mexican Mint (P2) and Silver Guest (P4) are absent, indicating a systemic loss. The lower pot now hosts an unidentified, singular sprout, which represents a new, uncatalogued introduction to the biome. The String of Nickels (P1) remains the most stable element of the collection.
